notes: Competent and popular figure mentioned by Ammianus Marcellinus, tasked with explaining sects like the Manicheans; originally named Strategius.

docs.title: BOOK XV
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: But at the same time, whenever he could find an opportunity, especially in any controversies or lawsuits (which is most shameful and wicked), he was greatly devoted to sordid gain.

docs.title: BOOK XVI
docs.author: Ammianus Marcellinus
docs.excerpt: … of many liberal accomplishments but corrupt, and a person easily turned from the truth by a bribe, acquired, by means of some emissaries who were skilful in deceiving and obtaining information, a knowledge of the plans of the Persians; taking to his counsels on this subject Cassianus, duke
